title: Primary Key and Physical Row Addresses
breadcrumbs: Primary Key and Physical Row Addresses
text: Primary Key and Physical Row Addresses A DBMS connector needs to uniquely identify each record, either by means of a primary key or a unique identification based on a physical access technique. Uniface adheres to the relational model, so every table accessed by Uniface must defined within Uniface as having a primary key. A record is uniquely identified by means of the field or fields that constitute the primary key. Uniface enforces the uniqueness of each record in a table via the primary key. Some DBMSs support the use of primary keys to uniquely identify each record. Connectors for these DBMSs use primary keys to locate records. Alternatively, some DBMSs support a unique identification based on a physical access technique. Uniface uses the generic term Physical Row Address (PRA) for this feature. Uniface is capable of using PRAs. Generally, using PRAs improves performance. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
